addressedFeedback.finding: Design accepted with no high or mid findings; implementation planning must cover productionRuntime, homebrewFormulaSource, allowsProductionCutover, TASK-009 review unblock, Swift production archives, formula rendering, smoke checks, and no publication side effects.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Created focused branch-local production cutover plan with archive, formula, gate-manifest, smoke, leakage, and release-handoff tasks.

addressedFeedback.finding: Initial plan let TASK-004 imply final production marker updates before TASK-005 local Homebrew smoke, and used an ad hoc formula-path brew install command instead of the documented local tap smoke flow.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Re-scoped TASK-004 to prepare evidence and docs while preserving top-level production markers until TASK-005 passes; replaced smoke commands with brew tap-new, tap formula render, brew install/test local/riela-test/riela, uninstall, and untap.

addressedFeedback.finding: Verification only built and checksummed darwin-arm64 even though accepted design and artifact contract require darwin-arm64 and darwin-x64 evidence or explicit blocked-target evidence before production marker transition.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Added darwin-x64 dry-run, build, tar payload, checksum, and gate-manifest evidence requirements; TASK-005 now keeps productionRuntime=typescript-bun, homebrewFormulaSource=bun-archive, and allowsProductionCutover=false when either macOS target is blocked.

addressedFeedback.finding: Parallelization guidance allowed TASK-002 and TASK-003 concurrency while task metadata marked both non-parallelizable.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Aligned narrative and PROGRESS.json by documenting TASK-002 and TASK-003 as sequential because builder outputs determine formula checksum inputs and both can touch packaging documentation.

addressedFeedback.finding: Accepted plan required branch-local production Homebrew packaging to switch from Bun archives to Swift executable archives after TASK-009 acceptance.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Implemented Swift production archive planning, builder cutover, macOS-only formula rendering, local Homebrew smoke, leakage checks, and cutover gate marker transition to swift-native / swift-executable-archive / allowsProductionCutover=true.

addressedFeedback.finding: Swift packaged CLI help and its regression test still said TypeScript/Bun remains the production runtime until cutover gates pass after production markers switched to Swift.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Updated rielaCLIHelpText to state Swift CLI is the production Homebrew runtime and Linux Homebrew remains unsupported until a reviewed Swift Linux build contract exists; updated testTopLevelHelpReturnsSuccessfulSmokeOutput to assert the new message and reject the stale TypeScript/Bun production fallback sentence.

addressedFeedback.finding: Per-module plan sections still showed NOT_STARTED and unchecked checklist rows despite completed task tables and progress log.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Changed per-module sections to COMPLETED and checked TASK-001 through TASK-005 checklist rows; added Step 7 review fix progress log entry.

addressedFeedback.finding: Formula test only asserted riela --help, allowing a Swift archive that rejects the required built-in add-on workflow usage smoke to pass brew test.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Added workflow usage parser and dispatch support, restored the formula test to create an addon-smoke workflow with riela/chat-reply-worker and assert workflow usage JSON output, added parser and CLI regression tests, rebuilt archives, and recorded archived binary workflow usage smoke evidence for both macOS targets.

addressedFeedback.finding: Packaged binary still presented as a migration CLI and narrow surface while formula/gates marked Swift as the production Homebrew runtime.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Changed --version to 0.1.15, changed help text to Riela CLI with Swift production Homebrew runtime wording, added workflow usage to the supported command list, and kept Linux Homebrew explicitly unsupported until a reviewed Swift Linux build contract exists.

addressedFeedback.finding: The restored Homebrew built-in add-on smoke assertion did not match Swift JSON output containing escaped slash `riela\/chat-reply-worker`, so brew test could fail while gate evidence recorded homebrewSmoke.status=passed and allowsProductionCutover=true.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Confirmed Formula/riela.rb renders `assert_match %r{riela\\?/chat-reply-worker}, usage` and scripts/render-homebrew-formula.sh emits the same escaped-slash-tolerant regex, reran local Homebrew install/test plus workflow usage smoke, and kept cutover gate evidence passed only after the smoke passed.

codexAgentReference: Preferred ../../codex-agent root unavailable; adjacent ../codex-agent remains reference-only. Use current Riela packaging scripts, TASK-009 evidence, Swift runtime tests, and pinned package contracts as behavior references; do not copy codex-agent source.
issueReference: tacogips/riela; issue title: Switch branch production packaging from Bun archives to Swift executable archives; no issueUrl or issueNumber supplied
parallelizableTasks: TASK-001 is parallelizable at plan start after completed cross-plan prerequisites.
TASK-002 and TASK-003 are sequential because the builder contract determines formula checksum inputs and both tasks can touch packaging documentation.
TASK-004 and TASK-005 are sequential because they mutate cutover gate evidence and plan progress state.
risks: Linux Homebrew remains unsupported until a reviewed Swift Linux build contract exists.
GitHub release upload and tacogips/homebrew-tap mutation were intentionally not performed by Step 6.
Homebrew smoke temporarily uninstalled the pre-existing riela formula, cleaned up the local tap, and restored tacogips/tap/riela 0.1.15 afterward.
